1. Spec guide section 6 amendments — reflect parent-prefix field naming and {source}_{verb}_{target} relationship-kind naming conventions established by SES-012 and applied here.
2. PI-006 sizing — decide whether governance-entity field-name retrofit is in v0.4 scope or deferred to v0.5+ (carries from SES-012).
3. PI-008 sizing — decide whether the in-app inbox folder watcher ships in v0.4 (eliminating apply_close_out.py for future conversations) or defers to v0.5+ (carries from SES-012).
4. Create-dialog domain-affiliation flow for entity panel — create-then-attach vs create-with-attach (new this conversation; carries to v0.4 build).
5. Cross-spec consistency check on inbound vocabulary once process.md and crm_candidate.md land — verify no relationship-kind name collisions.
6. Concurrent identifier-assignment behavior for entity (likely same pattern as domain build adopts).

Workstream progress: 2 of 4 schema-design conversations complete (domain ✓, entity ✓). Process and crm_candidate schema-design conversations queued. v0.4-build-planning conversation queued after all four schema specs exist.
after_payload.summary: Second of four schema-design conversations in the methodology entity schema-design workstream. Produced the entity schema specification (PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/entity.md, 452 lines) defining the entity entity type for v2 storage under minimum-viable v0.4 scope. Eight architectural questions resolved into five decisions (DEC-050 through DEC-054). Inherited and applied without re-decision the two cross-spec conventions established by SES-012 (parent-prefix field naming per DEC-046; {source}_{verb}_{target} relationship-kind naming per DEC-048). Registered entity_scopes_to_domain as the first formal cross-entity vocabulary entry in the methodology workstream — many-to-many via the universal references table. Two new planning items authored (PI-009 master-pane Domains column on Entities panel paired with PI-007 short codes; PI-010 entity-schema v0.5+ extensions covering variants and base-type/kind classification). Successor conversation: process schema design.
after_payload.title: entity schema design — methodology entity schema workstream conversation #2
after_payload.topics_covered:
Seed prompt: "Design the `entity` entity type schema for v2's storage layer. The 'entity' here is the CRM-modeled noun — Contact, Account, Session, Engagement, Dues — the things the eventual CRM stores records about. Drive a structured architectural discussion that produces one deliverable: `PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/entity.md` — the complete schema specification per the template in `methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide.md`. Ship thin in v0.4 — name, brief description, status — designed with growth path in mind for v0.5+ when Phase 3 demands attach fields, relationships, and full PRD content."

Pre-flight reading completed: crmbuilder/CLAUDE.md (root, including the v2 reference vocabulary mechanics at line 48); methodology-schema-workstream-plan.md (master, noting line 168 names ENT-NNN as the working assumption per DEC-043); methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide.md (template); methodology-schema-specs/domain.md v1.0 (predecessor spec, 379 lines, source of inherited conventions); phase-1-interview-guide.md v0.2 (line 62 entity-definition scope; section 7.2 Domain Inventory output spec excluding candidate entities; section 4.3 between-sessions discipline); close-out-payloads/ses_012.json (DEC-044..049 + PI-006/007/008 from the domain conversation; payload exists but db-export snapshots through SES-011/DEC-043 confirm it has not yet been applied — numbers in this payload assume SES-012 lands first); decisions.json snapshot (DEC-038/039/043 read in detail for context on derived-fields, minimum-viable inventory rationale, identifier-asymmetry resolution).

Eight architectural questions resolved in order:

Q1 — Identifier prefix and format. Affirmed ENT under domain.md's soft-3-letter posture (DEC-044). Three letters, unambiguous, no collision with existing prefixes (DEC, SES, RSK, PI, TOP, REF, CHR, STA, DOM), matches v2 governance-entity norm, already named as the working assumption in DEC-043 and workstream plan line 168. → DEC-050.

Q2 — Field inventory under minimum-viable v0.4 scope. Adopted a single content field (entity_description) rather than mirroring domain's two-content-field split (purpose + description). Reasoning: domain_purpose answers a priority-test mission question that has no analog for entity — an entity is a noun the client uses, and the 'why this entity matters to the engagement' question is answered relationally by its entity_scopes_to_domain references, not by free-text purpose. Adding entity_purpose would duplicate entity_description or invent a methodology concept Phase 1 doesn't produce. Five substantive fields plus inherited timestamps: entity_identifier, entity_name, entity_description, entity_notes (optional), entity_status. No entity_kind base-type classification in v0.4 (Phase 3 territory; deferred under PI-010). No storage-level length caps (UI soft guidance only, matching domain). Case-insensitive entity_name uniqueness within the engagement. Parent-prefix field naming inherited from DEC-046 without re-establishment. → DEC-051.

Q3 — Status lifecycle. Adopted domain's three-status pattern unchanged (candidate / confirmed / deferred), one-way propose-verify gate, freely bidirectional between confirmed and deferred, rejection via soft-delete, no archived status. Documented the entity-specific status-independence-from-affiliation-status posture: an entity's entity_status is its own field, set by the consultant based on client verification of the entity itself, not derived from the statuses of the domains the entity scopes to. This independence matters because an entity may legitimately span domains at different lifecycle positions (Contact in CBM might scope to MN/MR confirmed and FU deferred simultaneously, and Contact's own status is confirmed because the client agreed Contact is a thing in their world). Server-side transition validation returning HTTP 422 with invalid_status_transition error body, identical to domain. Noted as a future v0.5+ open question: a separate 'defined' or 'specified' status may surface once fields land, or the references-table cardinality between entity and field may suffice as the implicit signal. → DEC-052.

Q4 — Domain affiliation mechanism. The architecturally consequential question. Considered three candidates: (A) references-entity edge with new entity_scopes_to_domain relationship kind, (B) multi-value FK column on entity table, (C) direct single-value FK. (C) rejected outright — contradicts the methodology reality that entities span domains (CBM Contact lives in MN/MR/FU simultaneously). (B) considered but rejected — breaks discipline with v2's references-first posture (DEC-006), reverse queries require scanning all entity rows, FK validation reinvents what the references mechanism handles, and future relationship kinds on entity (entity-touches-process, entity-has-field, entity-has-variant) would each need their own multi-value columns, proliferating schema. Adopted (A). Many-to-many cardinality with no upper bound on either side. Zero-affiliation explicitly permitted (Phase 1 sometimes surfaces an entity name before its domain scoping is settled; the references discipline must not force pre-decided affiliation). Soft-deleting either endpoint does not cascade-delete references; restore restores them in place. The existing ReferencesSection widget renders the affiliations in the detail pane for free. → DEC-053 (combines mechanism and vocabulary registration since they are inseparable architectural choices).

Q5 — Relationship-kind vocabulary additions. Single new vocab entry: entity_scopes_to_domain (source=entity, target=domain). Conforms to the {source}_{verb}_{target} naming pattern inherited from DEC-048 — naming convention adopted not re-decided. Three coordinated mechanical additions per CLAUDE.md line 48: add entry to REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS in vocab.py; extend _kinds_for_pair so (entity, domain) returns {entity_scopes_to_domain}; Alembic migration extending the refs.relationship_kind CHECK constraint to include the new value. No other vocab additions in this spec; inbound kinds (process_touches_entity, field_belongs_to_entity, entity_variant_of_entity) are anticipated and documented informationally in section 3.3.2 but declared by their source-side specs (process.md and future v0.5+ specs), not by this one. → Folded into DEC-053.

Q6 — API surface. Adopted standard cross-spec endpoint set with no deviations (8 endpoints including /entities/next-identifier helper per DEC-043). Server-side status-transition validation identical to domain's pattern. Only ?include_deleted=true list filter in v0.4. Decomposed reference handling explicitly called out: domain affiliations are NOT inlined into entity create/update bodies; the client makes separate POST /references calls with source/target/kind. This keeps the entity API consistent with v2's references-first discipline (DEC-006) and matches how the v0.3 UI handles references for governance entities; the New dialog hides the two-call sequence behind a single user gesture but the API stays decomposed. No shortcut endpoints. → Folded into DEC-054.

Q7 — UI considerations. Adopted spec guide default ListDetailPanel layout with no architectural deviations. Methodology sidebar group position #2 (after Domains, before Processes and CRM Candidates which ship together in v0.4). Standard four-column master pane (identifier, name, status, updated). One real deferral: a Domains-column on the master pane was considered for v0.4 since entity-to-domain affiliation is the entity's defining classification, but deferred to v0.5+ paired with PI-007 short codes. Without short codes, the column would render DOM-001/002/003 — identifiers that mean nothing at a glance; with short codes (MN/MR/FU), the column becomes high-value. The N+1 / batched-join query design isn't free for a column whose value the detail pane already exposes one click away via ReferencesSection. Tracked as new PI-009. Detail pane shows fields in section-3.2 order with entity_notes under a collapsible 'Internal notes' header collapsed by default (mirrors domain). Create dialog's domain-affiliation flow (create-then-attach vs create-with-attach) left as an open question for v0.4-build planning to decide since both satisfy the acceptance criterion that the user can attach affiliations without leaving the UI. → Folded into DEC-054.

Q8 — Acceptance criteria. Drafted 16 numbered testable statements: 13 mirror domain's structural set (schema migration, identifier format constraint, name uniqueness, status enum/transition validation, access-layer methods, REST endpoint round-trip, identifier-auto-assignment concurrency, soft-delete/restore, sidebar entry, master pane columns, detail pane fields, CRUD dialogs, file-watch refresh) with entity field-name substitutions, plus three specifically covering the new vocabulary registration and references mechanism: (14) entity_scopes_to_domain registered in REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS and constrained correctly at _kinds_for_pair and refs.relationship_kind CHECK constraint; (15) references created and queryable bidirectionally with persistence across soft-delete/restore of either endpoint; (16) sample CBM-redo Phase 1 records authored through the UI including domain affiliations. → Folded into DEC-054.

Outline approved and end-to-end drafting executed without per-section confirmation. Spec committed to main at 40e2cf5. Consolidated eight question outcomes into five decisions (DEC-050 through DEC-054) — narrower than domain's six because the cross-spec conventions established by SES-012 (parent-prefix field naming per DEC-046; {source}_{verb}_{target} relationship-kind naming per DEC-048) carry forward unchanged and are adopted not re-decided in this spec.

Two new planning items authored: PI-009 master-pane Domains column on Entities panel (jointly enabled by PI-007 short codes); PI-010 entity-schema v0.5+ extensions covering variants (Mentor Contact / Client Contact pattern; mechanism TBD) and base-type/kind classification (person / organization / event / transaction). Tracked separately from PI-004 (which groups other deferred entity types) because these are entity-schema-growth, not new entity types.

after_payload.alternatives_considered: ENTITY (6 letters, verbose, breaks rhythm with existing prefixes). E (too short, ambiguous). EN (2 letters, breaks 3-5 convention). MENT for 'methodology entity' (verbose, unnecessary disambiguation since 'entity' is unambiguous in this context).
after_payload.consequences: entity.md uses ENT-NNN throughout. No collision with existing prefixes. Downstream process and crm_candidate specs retain the soft-3-letter flexibility preserved by domain.md; each justifies its choice in its own section 3.1. Cross-spec consistency check at v0.4-build planning verifies no prefix collisions across the four methodology entity types.
after_payload.context: The entity methodology entity needs an identifier prefix. The workstream plan line 168 and DEC-043 both name ENT-NNN as the working assumption. The predecessor domain.md established a soft-3-letter prefix posture: the v2 governance-entity norm is 3 letters (DEC, SES, RSK, TOP, REF, CHR, STA) and domain adopted DOM in keeping with that norm, but the spec guide allows 3-5 letters and downstream specs retain flexibility where 3 letters is ambiguous (e.g., PROC over PRC for clarity; CRMC over CRM to avoid product-name collision).
after_payload.decision: Adopt ENT as the identifier prefix for the entity methodology entity. Format ENT-NNN, zero-padded to 3 digits, server-assigned on POST omission via the GET /entities/next-identifier helper (per DEC-043 / SES-010 resolution).
after_payload.rationale: ENT is three letters, unambiguous, reads naturally as 'entity', has no collision with any existing prefix (DEC, SES, RSK, PI, TOP, REF, CHR, STA, DOM), and matches the existing v2 pattern visually. Adheres to domain.md's soft-3-letter posture without consuming any of the flexibility-budget the posture preserves for process and crm_candidate.
after_payload.title: entity identifier prefix and format

after_payload.alternatives_considered: Mirror domain's purpose+description split for cross-spec consistency (rejected as forcing a methodology concept that Phase 1 doesn't produce). Include entity_kind base-type classification in v0.4 (rejected as Phase 3 territory; tracked under PI-010). Storage-level length caps at v0.4 (rejected; caps are easy to add later and hard to relax once imposed; CBM-redo signal preferred for sizing). Markdown for entity_description (deferred to CBM-redo signal; matches domain's posture). Structured-journal pattern for entity_notes (deferred to CBM-redo signal; matches domain_notes posture).
after_payload.consequences: Eight-column entities table. CBM redo's Phase 1 captures entity records with the field set as specified; if real use surfaces methodology gaps (entity_purpose-shaped need, base-type classification need, length pathologies, markdown rendering pressure), they become v0.5+ candidates with concrete real-use evidence. Cross-spec naming convention from DEC-046 applied uniformly: even entity_identifier and timestamps carry the entity_ prefix for full convention consistency.
after_payload.context: DEC-039 explicitly justifies entity's inclusion in v0.4's minimum-viable inventory despite Phase 1 not formally producing an Entity Inventory output: 'real Phase 1 conversations naturally surface entity vocabulary (per the interview guide's line 62) and if v2 has no entity type those names land as loose nouns in session notes or process descriptions, forcing retrofit at the Phase 3 boundary.' DEC-039 names the working minimum as 'name, description, status'. The predecessor domain.md went further, splitting its content side into domain_purpose (the why — priority-test artifact) and domain_description (the what — kinds of work covered) plus optional domain_notes. The question is whether entity warrants the same split or simplifies to one description field.
after_payload.decision: Adopt a five-field shape under the parent-prefix convention inherited from DEC-046: entity_identifier (TEXT, server-assigned, ^ENT-\d{3}$, unique), entity_name (TEXT, required, non-empty trimmed, case-insensitive unique within the engagement), entity_description (TEXT, required, non-empty trimmed, single content field rather than purpose+description split), entity_notes (TEXT, optional, plain text, internal consultant scratchpad), entity_status (TEXT, required, default candidate, enum). Plus inherited timestamp fields (entity_created_at, entity_updated_at, entity_deleted_at) — eight columns total. No entity_kind base-type classification in v0.4 (deferred under PI-010). No storage-level length caps; UI placeholder text provides soft guidance only.
after_payload.rationale: Single entity_description rather than a purpose+description split because domain_purpose exists to capture the priority-test mission-relationship at domain granularity ('why does the mission require this domain?'), and entity has no comparable mission relationship. An entity is a noun the client uses; the 'why this entity matters to the engagement' question is answered relationally by the entity_scopes_to_domain references, not by free-text purpose. Adding entity_purpose would either duplicate entity_description or invent a methodology concept Phase 1 doesn't produce. entity_notes captures the same consultant-scratchpad role as domain_notes — pattern-library rationale, push-back trails, between-session reasoning. No length caps because caps are easy to add via migration later and hard to relax once imposed; UI soft guidance handles the typical case. No entity_kind because base-type semantics are Phase 3 / CRM-engine territory; v0.4's job is to capture Phase 1 surfacing, not to model CRM implementation specifics.
after_payload.title: entity field inventory and validation under minimum-viable v0.4 scope

after_payload.alternatives_considered: Add a fourth status value 'defined' or 'specified' to distinguish 'client agreed entity is in scope' from 'we have a full definition of this entity' (rejected for v0.4; v0.4 has no fields and the distinction has no expressive shape; revisited as a CBM-redo open question for v0.5+). Derive entity_status from the union of its affiliated-domain statuses (rejected as documented in section 3.4.3). Different transition rules from domain's (no reason to deviate surfaced).
after_payload.consequences: entity.md section 3.4 mirrors domain.md section 3.4 structurally with one additional subsection (3.4.3) on status independence. Cross-spec principle 'status tracks engagement-scope lifecycle; soft-delete tracks existence-in-the-record' applied consistently across domain and entity. CBM redo will surface whether the 'defined vs confirmed' distinction is needed for v0.5+; if so, either a fourth status value or the implicit signal from entity-to-field references' presence becomes the mechanism.
after_payload.context: DEC-047 established for domain a three-value lifecycle (candidate / confirmed / deferred) with default candidate starter, one-way propose-verify gate (no regression from confirmed or deferred back to candidate), bidirectional movement between confirmed and deferred, rejection via soft-delete, no archived status. The cross-spec principle 'status values track engagement-scope lifecycle; soft-delete tracks existence-in-the-record' was established. The question for entity is whether to mirror domain's lifecycle exactly or deviate, and whether entity-specific status semantics need additional documentation.
after_payload.decision: Adopt domain's three-value lifecycle unchanged. entity_status enum values candidate / confirmed / deferred with identical predecessor/successor transition rules. Default candidate on POST omission; explicit non-starter values permitted on POST for bulk-import scenarios. Server-side transition validation at the access layer returns HTTP 422 with invalid_status_transition envelope on bad transitions. Rejection via soft-delete; no archived status. Add one entity-specific subsection (3.4.3) documenting that entity_status is independent of any affiliated-domain statuses — an entity may legitimately span domains at different lifecycle positions and its own status is set by consultant judgment based on client verification of the entity itself, not derived from references.
after_payload.rationale: Entities, like domains, are surfaced by the consultant and verified by the client; the propose-verify dynamic is the same, so the lifecycle pattern transfers without modification. The status-independence-from-affiliation-status subsection matters because the temptation to derive entity status from the union of its domain affiliations' statuses is real but wrong: Contact in CBM might scope to MN (confirmed), MR (confirmed), FU (deferred) simultaneously, and Contact's own status is confirmed because the client agreed Contact is a thing in their world. Coupling the two would hide that client decision behind a derivation rule. Explicit documentation prevents either (a) future maintainers from introducing the derivation, or (b) downstream specs from inferring different semantics.
after_payload.title: entity status lifecycle (adopts domain pattern; documents status-independent-of-affiliation-status posture)

after_payload.alternatives_considered: Option B (multi-value FK column on entity, e.g., entity_domain_identifiers as JSON array). Option C (direct single-value entity_domain_identifier FK). Hybrid pattern with one 'primary domain' FK plus references for additional affiliations (rejected as inventing complexity without value — CBM's Contact doesn't have a primary domain, it's genuinely shared). Different relationship-kind naming (e.g., target-first 'domain_contains_entity', or generic 'scopes_to'; rejected per DEC-048's source-first convention and disambiguation benefit of explicit source/target naming).
after_payload.consequences: Refs table grows by the count of entity-to-domain edges across the engagement. ReferencesSection widget renders affiliations in the entity detail pane for free; same widget renders inbound entity references from the domain side. Cross-spec consistency check at v0.4-build planning verifies the source-side mechanism choices align across the four methodology entity types. The choice establishes a pattern for downstream specs: when a methodology entity has a many-to-many relationship to another methodology entity, the references-entity edge is the default mechanism unless there's a substantive reason to deviate.
after_payload.context: domain.md section 3.3.2 named many-to-many via the references entity as the working assumption for entity-to-domain affiliation, citing CBM's Contact-shared-across-MN/MR/FU pattern. The mechanism choice belongs to entity.md as the source-side spec. Three candidate mechanisms exist in v2: (A) references-entity edge with new entity_scopes_to_domain relationship kind, (B) multi-value FK column on the entity table, (C) direct single-value FK on the entity table. The references infrastructure (DEC-006) is the established cross-entity-type edge store; the question is whether to use it for entity-to-domain or to introduce a new pattern.
after_payload.decision: Adopt option A: references-entity edge with new relationship kind entity_scopes_to_domain. Mechanical additions per CLAUDE.md line 48: (1) add entity_scopes_to_domain to REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS in vocab.py; (2) extend _kinds_for_pair so (entity, domain) returns {entity_scopes_to_domain} (the only kind for this pair in v0.4); (3) Alembic migration extending the refs.relationship_kind CHECK constraint to include the new value. Many-to-many cardinality with no upper bound on either side. Zero-affiliation explicitly permitted in v0.4. Source must be a live entity record; target must be a live domain record (existing access-layer rules). Duplicate (source_id, target_id, relationship_kind) tuples rejected by the references-table uniqueness constraint. Soft-deleting either endpoint does not cascade-delete references; restore restores them in place. Vocabulary entry conforms to the {source}_{verb}_{target} naming pattern inherited from DEC-048.
after_payload.rationale: Option C (direct single-value FK) rejected outright — contradicts the methodology reality that entities span domains. Option B (multi-value FK on entity table) considered but rejected — breaks discipline with v2's references-first posture (DEC-006), reverse queries ('what entities scope to this domain?') require scanning all entity rows, FK validation reinvents what the references mechanism already handles, and future relationship kinds on entity (entity-touches-process, entity-has-field, entity-has-variant) would each need their own multi-value columns, proliferating schema for what the references entity exists to absorb. Option A reuses existing v2 infrastructure (refs table, vocab.py, Alembic migration pattern, ReferencesSection widget) and matches how the v0.3 desktop UI handles references for governance entities. Zero-affiliation permitted because Phase 1 sometimes surfaces entity names before domain scoping is settled and the references discipline must not force pre-decided affiliation.
after_payload.title: entity-to-domain affiliation mechanism via references-entity edge + entity_scopes_to_domain vocabulary registration

after_payload.alternatives_considered: Ship the Domains column in v0.4 rendering raw DOM-NNN identifiers (rejected as poor UX without short codes; better to ship the column alongside PI-007). Decide the create-dialog flow inline in this spec (rejected as UI-implementation question better answered with cross-cutting v0.4-build visibility). Inline-affiliation endpoint as a convenience shortcut (rejected as inventing API surface that references-table API already covers; decomposed pattern is more consistent). Additional server-side list filters in v0.4 (deferred to CBM-redo signal; client-side filtering is sufficient at the expected entity count). Different sidebar position for Entities (no reason to deviate from workstream-order surfaced).
after_payload.consequences: entity.md sections 3.5 / 3.6 / 3.7 follow the established patterns with explicit calls on the deferred Domains column and open create-dialog flow. v0.4-build-planning conversation has to decide the create-dialog flow as part of UI implementation. CBM redo will validate (a) whether the Domains-column deferral is right (low-friction without it, or genuine pain point); (b) whichever create-dialog flow is implemented (is the create-then-attach two-gesture sequence acceptable, or is multi-select-on-create needed). PI-009 added as a v0.5+ planning item. Cross-spec consistency check at v0.4-build planning verifies sidebar ordering and panel layout patterns align across all four methodology entity types.
after_payload.context: domain.md adopted the spec guide's default 8-endpoint API surface and the default ListDetailPanel UI layout without deviations. For entity, the same defaults fit cleanly, but two entity-specific UI questions need explicit decisions: (a) whether to add a Domains column to the master pane (entity-to-domain affiliation is entity's defining classification in v0.4), and (b) what the create-dialog domain-affiliation flow looks like (create-then-attach via the detail pane vs create-with-attach via a multi-select in the dialog itself).
after_payload.decision: Adopt standard 8-endpoint API surface with no deviations: GET /entities, GET/PUT/PATCH/DELETE /entities/{id}, POST /entities, POST /entities/{id}/restore, GET /entities/next-identifier. Server-side status-transition validation per the domain pattern. Only ?include_deleted=true list filter in v0.4. Decomposed reference handling: domain affiliations NOT inlined into entity create/update bodies; client makes separate POST /references calls. Default ListDetailPanel UI in the existing Methodology sidebar group (position #2 after Domains). Standard four-column master pane (identifier, name, status, updated) — defer a Domains column to v0.5+ paired with PI-007 short codes, tracked as new PI-009. Detail pane shows fields in section-3.2 order with entity_notes under a collapsible 'Internal notes' header collapsed by default (mirrors domain). Edge-text confirmation on delete. Create-dialog domain-affiliation flow (create-then-attach vs create-with-attach) left as an open question for the v0.4-build-planning conversation to decide. 16 testable acceptance criteria covering schema migration, identifier format constraint, name uniqueness, status enum/transition validation, access-layer methods, REST endpoint round-trip, identifier-auto-assignment concurrency, soft-delete/restore, sidebar entry, master pane columns, detail pane fields, CRUD dialogs, file-watch refresh, plus three specific to the new vocabulary registration and references mechanism.
after_payload.rationale: No-deviation default endpoints minimize cross-spec complexity and reuse existing v2 patterns. Decomposed reference handling keeps the entity API consistent with v2's references-first discipline (DEC-006); shortcut endpoints would invent API surface area that the references-table API already covers. Domains column deferred to v0.5+ for two reasons: (a) without PI-007 short codes, the column would render DOM-001/002/003 — identifiers that mean nothing at a glance, so the column's UX value is tied to PI-007 landing; (b) the batched-join query design isn't free for a column whose value the detail pane already exposes one click away via ReferencesSection. Shipping the column alongside PI-007 keeps both deliverables aligned. Create-dialog flow left open because both candidate patterns satisfy the acceptance criterion that the user can attach affiliations without leaving the UI; the choice is UI-implementation, not schema-design, and the v0.4-build-planning conversation has the right cross-cutting visibility to decide.
after_payload.title: entity API surface, UI defaults, deferred Domains-column posture, acceptance criteria for v0.4

after_payload.description: DEC-054 defers a Domains column on the master pane of the Entities panel to v0.5+, jointly enabled by PI-007 (domain.short_code field). The column renders affiliated domains as comma-separated short codes (e.g., 'MN, MR, FU') once short codes exist; without them, DOM-NNN identifiers don't tell a consultant anything at a glance and the column's UX value is lost. Implementation scope when both PI-007 and PI-009 land together: (a) access-layer batched-join query (list_entities_with_affiliations() or equivalent) returning each entity's affiliated-domain short codes alongside its scalar fields without N+1 queries; (b) ListDetailPanel view-model update to surface the new column; (c) column-width and sorting behavior decisions (sort by what? probably alphabetical-by-short-code-list with secondary sort by entity_name). Prioritization signal: CBM redo will reveal whether the absence of the column in v0.4 is high-friction (do consultants frequently click into the detail pane to check affiliations? do they request a list view grouped by domain?). If high-friction, PI-009 + PI-007 move up in v0.5 priority; if low-friction, they stay queued.
after_payload.title: Master-pane Domains column on the Entities panel (paired with PI-007 short codes)

after_payload.description: DEC-051 and DEC-053 jointly defer two entity-schema growth areas to v0.5+: (a) entity variants — the Mentor Contact / Client Contact pattern from CBM history where two related-but-distinct entities share a base type but carry different fields and role semantics; (b) entity base-type / kind classification — person / organization / event / transaction-style categorization that informs CRM-engine selection and field-shape defaults. Both are intentionally absent from v0.4: variants because Phase 1 doesn't produce them and v0.4 has no fields to vary across them; base-type/kind because it's Phase 3 territory tied to CRM-engine specifics that v0.4's thin shape stays neutral about. Tracked separately from PI-004 (which groups field/requirement/manual_config/test_spec as new methodology entity types) because PI-010 is entity-schema growth — extending the existing entity type — not introducing new entity types. Variant mechanism TBD: candidates include self-referential FK (entity_parent_identifier), references-entity edge (entity_variant_of_entity), or a separate variant entity type sitting between base entity and field. Mechanism choice is a v0.5+ design conversation. Workaround in v0.4: variants are distinguished by suffixing entity_name ('Contact — Mentor', 'Contact — Client'); CBM redo will reveal how much friction the workaround creates and inform mechanism design. Base-type/kind classification: candidate v0.5+ shape is an enum field (entity_kind: person | organization | event | transaction | other) on the entity table, with validation that informs Phase 3 field-shape defaults and Phase 5 CRM-evaluation scoring.
after_payload.title: Entity-schema v0.5+ extensions — variants and base-type/kind classification

after_payload.artifacts_produced:
PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/process.md (the process methodology entity schema specification, v1.0, 499 lines).

Five decisions:
- DEC-055: process identifier prefix and format (PROC, four letters via soft-3-letter posture deviation)
- DEC-056: process field inventory under v0.4 scope (three content fields, no description-distinct-from-purpose, engagement-global name uniqueness, no status field deviation)
- DEC-057: process_classification field (four-value enum, unclassified default, one-way unclassified gate with free movement among classified values, replaces working name process_priority)
- DEC-058: process relationship architecture (direct FK for domain, references-entity directional edge for process_hands_off_to_process, defer process-to-entity)
- DEC-059: process API surface, UI defaults, acceptance criteria (standard endpoints, position #3 in Methodology sidebar, Domain column deferred to v0.5+, 15 acceptance criteria)

One new planning item:
- PI-011: future scalar implementation-priority field for ranking within classification buckets

Two paste-ready prompts produced as conversation content (not committed files; queued for use in a separate Claude.ai session): preferences-revision primary prompt defining detail / architecture / prototype modes; preferences-revision follow-on prompt establishing the architecture-mode decision template.
after_payload.conversation_reference: Claude.ai schema-design conversation that produced PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/process.md on 05-12-26. Third of four schema-design conversations in the methodology entity schema-design workstream established by SES-011. No transcript preserved per DEC-025.
after_payload.in_flight_at_end:
Two parallel queued workstreams.

Main workstream: next conversation is crm_candidate schema design (fourth and final schema-design conversation in the methodology entity schema-design workstream). Kickoff at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/schema-design-kickoff-crm_candidate.md. The crm_candidate conversation inherits the conventions established and applied across SES-012 / SES-013 / SES-014 (parent-prefix field naming, {source}_{verb}_{target} relationship-kind naming, soft-3-letter prefix posture with explicit four-letter deviation when warranted, rejection-via-soft-delete, no-archived, decomposed reference handling, engagement-global case-insensitive name uniqueness). After crm_candidate completes, the v0.4-build-planning conversation takes all four schema specs as input and produces the v0.4 PRD, implementation plan, and slice build prompts. Cross-spec consistency check runs at the start of the v0.4-build-planning conversation, verifying no vocab-kind name collisions and that conventions are applied consistently.

Parallel workstream: preferences-revision session queued. The user will run two paste-ready prompts in a separate Claude.ai session before opening the crm_candidate conversation: (1) primary prompt defining detail / architecture / prototype modes with cadence rules and default-mode declaration mechanism; (2) follow-on prompt establishing the architecture-mode decision template. Output: revised userPreferences, project-level instructions, and kickoff-prompt template additions. The schema-design-kickoff-crm_candidate.md file should be updated with an 'Operating mode: architecture' (or 'prototype') line at the top once the preferences-revision lands, so the cadence is explicit before the next conversation opens.
after_payload.summary: Third of four schema-design conversations in the methodology entity schema-design workstream. Produced the process schema specification (PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/process.md, 499 lines) defining the process entity type for v2 storage under minimum-viable v0.4 scope. Architectural decisions resolved into five DEC records (DEC-055 through DEC-059). Inherited the cross-spec conventions established by SES-012 and applied by SES-013 (parent-prefix field naming, {source}_{verb}_{target} relationship-kind naming, soft-3-letter prefix posture with explicit deviation clause, rejection-via-soft-delete) and explicitly deviated on one structural point: process does not have a status field in v0.4. The deviation rationale — domain/entity use status for engagement-scope lifecycle, process uses process_classification for that role, and a definitional-completeness lifecycle field becomes meaningful only when v0.5+ adds Phase 3 detail levels — is documented in spec section 3.4.1. Registered process_hands_off_to_process as the second formal cross-entity vocabulary entry in the methodology workstream, directional (source = producer, target = consumer). Deferred process-to-entity touches entirely (no vocab registration in v0.4) under PI-005. One new planning item authored (PI-011 future scalar implementation-priority field). Mid-conversation, identified working-cadence calibration issues with the conversation's own dialog mode and produced two paste-ready prompts for a separate Claude.ai session to update userPreferences and project-level instructions: a primary prompt establishing detail / architecture / prototype mode definitions, and a follow-on prompt establishing the architecture-mode decision template (eight elements: plain-language question, concrete example, options with concrete behavior, why it matters, cost, recommendation, follow-on detail, decision request). The preferences-revision work is queued for a separate session before the next CRMBuilder conversation. Successor conversation: crm_candidate schema design.
after_payload.title: process schema design — methodology entity schema workstream conversation #3
after_payload.topics_covered:
Seed prompt: "Design the process entity type schema for v2's storage layer. A 'process' in the methodology sense is a sequence of work the organization does to advance its mission — Client Intake, Mentor Onboarding, Annual Dues Cycle, etc. — the things that get full Process Documents in Phase 3 of the evolved methodology and that constitute the Prioritized Backbone in Phase 1. Drive a structured architectural discussion that produces one deliverable: PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/process.md — the complete schema specification per the template in methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide.md."

Pre-flight reading completed: crmbuilder/CLAUDE.md (root, including the v2 reference vocabulary mechanics at line 48); methodology-schema-workstream-plan.md (master); methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide.md (template); methodology-schema-specs/domain.md v1.0 (first predecessor, 379 lines, source of inherited conventions); methodology-schema-specs/entity.md v1.0 (second predecessor, 452 lines, source of references-decomposition pattern and master-pane-column-deferral pattern); close-out-payloads/ses_012.json (DEC-044..049 + PI-006/007/008 from domain, applied); close-out-payloads/ses_013.json (DEC-050..054 + PI-009/010 from entity, payload exists but not yet applied per db-export snapshot — numbers in this SES-014 payload assume SES-013 lands first); phase-1-interview-guide.md v0.2 (section 3.5 Part C process surfacing; section 4.1 between-session drafting with workability check; section 7.3 Prioritized Backbone output spec); evolved-methodology-phase-outline.md (Principle 3 priority-at-process-level; Principle 7 priority-not-locked; Phase 1 / Phase 3 boundary).

Architectural questions resolved:

Q1 — Identifier prefix and format. Adopted PROC (four letters) invoking domain.md's soft-3-letter posture explicit deviation clause. PRC rejected for ambiguity (reads as typo, no natural disambiguation). PR collides with public relations and is two letters. P too short. No collision with DEC/SES/RSK/PI/TOP/REF/CHR/STA/DOM/ENT. → DEC-055.

Q2 — Priority vs. status: two fields or one. The kickoff prompt's working set (candidate / confirmed / deprecated / deferred) conflated two orthogonal concepts: methodology priority classification (per Principle 3) and propose-verify record lifecycle. After clarifying that the user reframed the lifecycle question as definitional-completeness rather than existence-verification ("in Phase 1 a process is identified and an overview provided; in later phases the process is refined / detailed / confirmed to be complete"), resolved that v0.4 schema has only the identified level and therefore no status field — a status field with one value is no field at all. The methodology's deferred semantic that status carries for domain and entity is captured for process by classification=deferred instead; overloading both fields would create field-collision ambiguity. Rejection continues to be handled by soft-delete per cross-spec precedent. → contributes to DEC-056 (no-status deviation) and DEC-057 (classification field).

Q3 — Field inventory under minimum-viable v0.4 scope. Three substantive content fields: process_purpose (required, one-sentence — explicitly produced in Phase 1 7.3 output for backbone processes), process_classification_rationale (optional — priority-test answer for mission-critical; one-line reason for supporting and deferred), process_notes (optional, internal scratchpad). No separate process_description (purpose carries the work-content meaning). No process_kind base-type classification in v0.4 (Phase 3 territory; deferred under PI-005). No storage-level length caps. Engagement-global case-insensitive uniqueness on process_name. Direct FK to domain via process_domain_identifier (required). → contributes to DEC-056.

Q4 — Process classification: term and values. User pushed back on the working name 'priority' because conventional priorities are scalar (Critical/High/Medium/Low) while the methodology's three buckets (mission-critical / supporting / deferred) are a fused classification mixing importance, scope, and role rather than a scalar. Adopted process_classification as the field name and unclassified | mission_critical | supporting | deferred as the enum values, with unclassified default reflecting the Session-1-capture-before-classification state. One-way out of unclassified; free movement among the three classified values per Principle 7. Future scalar implementation-priority field deferred and tracked as a new PI. → DEC-057. Future scalar tracked as PI-011.

Q5 — Domain affiliation mechanism. Direct FK column on process table (process_domain_identifier), not references-table edge. Cardinality settled at the methodology level (many-to-one). No vocab.py registration — the references vocab governs the references table only, and a direct FK doesn't produce references rows. Domain.md section 3.3.2's anticipated relationship name (process_belongs_to_domain) stays informational only. → contributes to DEC-058.

Q6 — Process-to-process connections: directional or undirected, name. Directional, per the methodology's handoff language in Phase 1 guide section 4.1 ("Process A hands off to Process B if A produces records or state that B requires") and section 7.3 output (handoffs to/from other backbone processes — both sides named distinctly). Source = producer; target = consumer. Relationship kind name: process_hands_off_to_process. Registered in REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS and _kinds_for_pair; Alembic migration extends CHECK constraint. → contributes to DEC-058.

Q7 — Process-to-entity touches in v0.4: defer entirely or register vocab now. Deferred entirely. PI-005 (process schema growth beyond Phase 1 thin shape) already tracks this. Pre-committing to a coarse vocab name (process_touches_entity) risks ossifying the wrong name when Phase 3 may need finer-grained kinds (read / create / update / delete). The spec's anticipated-inbound-relationships table (section 3.3.2) lists process_touches_entity as v0.5+ vocab without registration. → contributes to DEC-058.

Q8 — Process name uniqueness: per-(domain, name) or engagement-global. Initially proposed per-(domain, name) to permit natural client-language names like 'Recruit' in both MR and CR domains. User pushed back asking what documentation would show for a per-domain unique name and applied the principle 'if there could ever be user confusion, then we should go engagement specific.' The handoff lists in Phase 1 output documents reference process names alone without domain context, creating ambiguity risk under per-domain uniqueness. Adopted engagement-global case-insensitive uniqueness mirroring domain/entity precedent; consultants use suffixed names (Mentor Recruit, Client Recruit) as CBM already does today. → contributes to DEC-056.

Q9 — API surface, UI defaults, acceptance criteria. Standard endpoint set with no deviations. Decomposed reference handling for handoffs (no inline-handoff convenience endpoints), matching entity.md decomposed posture for affiliations. UI: Methodology sidebar group position #3 (Domains, Entities, Processes, CRM Candidates); master pane columns Identifier / Name / Classification / Updated (Domain column deferred to v0.5+ paired with PI-007, mirroring entity.md's posture); detail pane renders bidirectional process_hands_off_to_process references in separate Hands-off-to and Receives-from sub-sections; create-dialog handoff flow (create-then-attach vs create-with-attach) left as v0.4-build decision matching the open question on entity affiliations. 15 testable acceptance criteria. → DEC-059.

Mid-conversation working-cadence calibration. Identified that the conversation's own dialog mode (per-section permission requests on every architectural sub-question, then over-correcting to flat-weight consolidated proposals without per-decision depth) was not serving the user. Produced two paste-ready prompts to be run in a separate session to update userPreferences, project-level instructions, and kickoff-prompt templates: (1) a primary prompt defining three named modes (detail / architecture / prototype) with cadence rules and a default mode declaration mechanism; (2) a follow-on prompt establishing the architecture-mode decision template — eight elements: plain-language question, concrete example, each option with concrete behavior, why it matters, cost of recommendation honestly named, recommendation with rationale, follow-on detail separated, decision request with named options. The two prompts together provide the long-term fix; the user runs them in a separate session before the next CRMBuilder conversation.

after_payload.alternatives_considered: PRC (three letters, reads as typo, rejected). PR (two letters, collides with public relations, rejected). P (one letter, too short, rejected). PROCESS (seven letters, verbose, breaks rhythm with other prefixes, rejected).
after_payload.consequences: process.md uses PROC-NNN throughout. No collision with existing prefixes. The crm_candidate spec conversation retains the soft-3-letter posture's flexibility-budget and decides its own prefix in its own section 3.1 (working assumption from SES-011 workstream plan was CRM but is potentially ambiguous against product-name; may end up as CRMC or similar). Cross-spec consistency check at v0.4-build planning verifies no prefix collisions across the four methodology entity types.
after_payload.context: The process methodology entity needs an identifier prefix. Per domain.md section 3.1 the soft-3-letter posture preserves flexibility for downstream methodology entity types to deviate to four letters when the three-letter form is ambiguous; the example given was PROC over PRC for clarity.
after_payload.decision: Adopt PROC as the identifier prefix for the process methodology entity. Format PROC-NNN, zero-padded to 3 digits, server-assigned on POST omission via the GET /processes/next-identifier helper (per DEC-043 / SES-010 resolution).
after_payload.rationale: PROC reads unambiguously as 'process' and avoids the genuine readability problem with PRC. The three-letter candidate PRC reads as a typo or an obscure acronym; consultants would have to mentally re-expand it every time. PR collides with public relations (which a CRM context might legitimately encounter as a domain or department) and is only two letters anyway. P is too short. PROC has no collision with existing prefixes (DEC, SES, RSK, PI, TOP, REF, CHR, STA, DOM, ENT). The four-letter form invokes domain.md's explicit deviation clause; no precedent is broken.
after_payload.title: process identifier prefix and format

after_payload.alternatives_considered: Per-(domain, name) name uniqueness (rejected for downstream documentation rendering ambiguity). Two-content-field split mirroring domain (purpose + description, rejected because for process both questions collapse to one). Three-value status field mirroring domain/entity (rejected because the methodology's process lifecycle is definitional, not propose-verify, and v0.4 has only one definitional level). Conditional-required process_classification_rationale (rejected as schema complexity; optional with UI placeholder cuing is simpler).
after_payload.consequences: process.md schema in v0.4 has eight substantive fields plus inherited timestamps. Cross-spec consistency check at v0.4-build planning verifies the deviation rationale is acceptable (it is — the spec guide section 6 permits deviation with explicit justification, and the deviation is explicitly justified). When v0.5+ introduces process schema growth (PI-005), a definitional-completeness lifecycle field returns. Future scalar implementation-priority is tracked separately as PI-011.
after_payload.context: The process methodology entity must host the Phase 1 Prioritized Backbone output (section 7.3 of phase-1-interview-guide.md): per process, a client-language name, a one-sentence purpose, a domain affiliation, a priority classification, the reasoning behind the classification, and the handoffs to/from other backbone processes. Phase 1 explicitly does not produce detailed process definitions; that's Phase 3 territory deferred under PI-005. The minimum-viable v0.4 schema captures only what Phase 1 produces.
after_payload.decision: Adopt a thin field inventory: process_identifier, process_name (required, engagement-global case-insensitive unique), process_domain_identifier (required, direct FK to domain.domain_identifier), process_purpose (required, one-sentence), process_classification_rationale (optional, classification reasoning text), process_notes (optional, internal scratchpad), process_classification (separate decision, see DEC-057), plus inherited timestamps. No separate process_description (one purpose field carries the work-content meaning for process the way two fields don't need to). No process_kind / base-type classification. No storage-level length caps. No process_status field — a deviation from domain.md / entity.md precedent — because the methodology's lifecycle on process is about definitional completeness, not propose-verify scope, and v0.4 schema has only the identified level (a status field with one value is no field at all). The engagement-scope information that domain and entity carry via status=deferred is carried for process by process_classification=deferred instead.
after_payload.rationale: Engagement-global name uniqueness was initially proposed as per-(domain, name) unique to allow natural client-language names like 'Recruit' in both Mentor Recruitment and Client Recruiting domains. The user pushed back applying the principle 'if there could ever be user confusion, then we should go engagement specific' — process handoff lists in Phase 1 output documents reference process names alone without domain context, creating ambiguity risk under per-domain uniqueness. Engagement-global mirrors precedent and matches CBM's existing pattern of suffixing names (Mentor Recruit, Client Recruit). The no-status-field deviation rationale: domain and entity use status to track propose-verify engagement scope; process has process_classification serving exactly that role (a process with classification=deferred is acknowledged-but-out-of-current-scope, same semantic as status=deferred for domain/entity). Adding a separate status field would either duplicate the meaning or introduce field-collision ambiguity (which 'deferred' applies?). A definitional-completeness lifecycle field (working name process_definition_level) returns in v0.5+ when PI-005 introduces detailed-process schema content with multiple completeness levels distinguishable in the schema. Single-content-field choice (purpose, no separate description) follows the same reasoning entity.md applied — for process, both questions reduce to 'what does this process do?' and one field handles it without information loss.
after_payload.title: process field inventory under minimum-viable v0.4 scope, with no-status-field deviation and engagement-global name uniqueness

after_payload.alternatives_considered: process_priority as field name (rejected — semantically misleading per user feedback). process_priority with values Critical/High/Medium/Low replacing the three-bucket classification (rejected — would diverge from Phase 1 7.3 output specification which explicitly produces Backbone / Supporting / Deferred sections by name; methodology change should precede schema change). process_tier as field name (acceptable alternative but classification is closer to the methodology's actual term). Required-no-default classification (rejected — incompatible with Phase 1 Session 1 capture cadence). Free regression to unclassified after classification (rejected — same rationale as domain's one-way propose-verify gate: post-decision state is meaningful and should be re-expressed via the classified values rather than abandoned).
after_payload.consequences: process records start unclassified by default; consultants classify between Session 1 and Session 2. The Phase 1 output document at end of Session 2 has zero unclassified processes (or any remaining unclassified are flagged as work-in-progress). A future scalar implementation-priority field (PI-011) attaches separately when Phase 2 Slice Planning content lands in v0.5+; this field carries methodology classification, that one carries implementation ranking. Cross-spec consistency check confirms process is the only methodology entity with a classification field in v0.4 (domain and entity have no analog).
after_payload.context: Principle 3 of the evolved methodology says priority is established at the process level and inherited downward; mission-criticality is the consistent organizing principle. Phase 1 produces three priority buckets in its Prioritized Backbone output: Backbone (mission-critical), Supporting, Deferred. Between Session 1 (process names captured from client conversation) and the between-session drafting work that classifies them, processes exist in an unclassified state — the Phase 1 guide explicitly says 'do not yet propose mission-critical classification — that's between-session work' (line 237).
after_payload.decision: Adopt process_classification as the field name (not process_priority — the user identified the methodology's three-bucket classification as conceptually distinct from a conventional scalar priority and pushed back on the priority terminology). Enum values: unclassified (default starter), mission_critical, supporting, deferred. Transitions: one-way out of unclassified (no regression to unclassified once classified); free bidirectional movement among the three classified values per Principle 7 ('decisions inside shipped iterations are locked; priority is not'). Server-side classification-transition validation per section 3.5.3 returns HTTP 422 on invalid transitions with {"error": "invalid_classification_transition", "from": ..., "to": ...}.
after_payload.rationale: Field naming: the user observed that conventional priority semantics imply a scalar (Critical/High/Medium/Low) ranking, whereas mission-critical/supporting/deferred is a classification fused with scope decisions and role descriptions. Calling the field 'priority' would misrepresent its semantic. 'classification' is more accurate and matches the methodology's actual semantic (a methodology classification fused with engagement-scope information). Default unclassified reflects the Phase 1 cadence faithfully — Session 1 captures names without classifying; between-session work classifies; Session 2 verifies the classifications. Making classification required-on-create would either block Session 1 capture or force premature pattern-matching. One-way out of unclassified protects the classification work as a meaningful engagement event; demoting a classified process should change to deferred rather than regress to unclassified. Free movement among classified values supports Principle 7's explicit allowance for cross-iteration priority review.
after_payload.title: process_classification field with four-value enum and unclassified default

after_payload.alternatives_considered: Domain via references-entity edge with new vocab kind (rejected — references mechanism is for many-to-many, not many-to-one with settled cardinality). Process-to-process undirected (rejected — methodology language is directional and would create rendering ambiguity). Process-to-process named process_connects_to_process or process_feeds_process (rejected — hands_off_to matches the methodology guide's literal word). Pre-register process_touches_entity in v0.4 with no UI to create the edges (rejected — risks ossifying the wrong name when Phase 3 may need read/create/update/delete decomposition). Pre-register finer-grained process_reads_entity / process_creates_entity / process_updates_entity / process_deletes_entity in v0.4 (rejected — premature commitment without Phase 3 design work).
after_payload.consequences: process table has process_domain_identifier FK column; processes always belong to exactly one live domain. process_hands_off_to_process registered in REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS and _kinds_for_pair; Alembic migration extends refs.relationship_kind CHECK constraint. process_belongs_to_domain stays a conceptual relationship name only — referenced informationally but not in vocab. Process-to-entity vocab waits for v0.5+ work under PI-005. Cross-spec consistency check confirms no name collision between process_hands_off_to_process and entity_scopes_to_domain (clearly distinct).
after_payload.context: Process is the most relational of the four methodology entity types. It belongs to one domain (many-to-one), connects to other process records through workflow handoffs (many-to-many), and is anticipated to touch entity records in v0.5+ once Phase 3 work demands. The mechanism for each connection (direct FK column vs references-entity edge) is decided per the spec guide section 3.3 case framework: direct FK when cardinality is settled and there's no edge metadata; references-entity edge when many-to-many or when metadata attaches to the edge.
after_payload.decision: Three architectural sub-decisions: (1) Domain affiliation via direct FK column (process_domain_identifier on the processes table). Many-to-one cardinality is settled at the methodology level (a process belongs to exactly one domain — Phase 1's Prioritized Backbone groups processes by domain). No vocab.py registration because the references vocab governs the references table only and direct FK doesn't produce references rows. (2) Process-to-process handoffs via references-entity edge with new vocabulary kind process_hands_off_to_process. Directional (source = producer, target = consumer) reflecting Phase 1 guide section 4.1 language: 'Process A hands off to Process B if A produces records or state that B requires to do its work.' Many-to-many cardinality (each process can hand off to many and receive from many). Self-loops technically valid but unusual. Bidirectional handoffs (feedback loops) require two distinct edges (A → B and B → A); the references uniqueness constraint prevents duplicates of identical (source, target, kind) tuples. (3) Process-to-entity touches deferred entirely. No vocab registration in v0.4. The anticipated v0.5+ kind (process_touches_entity, possibly decomposed into read/create/update/delete variants) lists informationally in section 3.3.2 but is not added to REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS or _kinds_for_pair. Phase 1 does not formally produce this data; Phase 3 may need finer-grained kinds that a pre-committed coarse name would ossify.
after_payload.rationale: Domain FK choice: cardinality is settled (per the methodology), edge has no metadata, lookup is trivial via FK. References-entity edge would be overkill and inconsistent with entity.md's many-to-many entity_scopes_to_domain only because the underlying cardinalities differ. Directional process-to-process: methodology language is inherently directional ('hands off'); Phase 1 output (section 7.3) names both directions distinctly per process ('handoffs to/from other backbone processes'). Undirected representation would force the consultant to track direction outside the system and force renderers to derive direction at display time. Cost of directional: feedback loops require two edges, but feedback loops are rare in Phase 1 backbones. Process-to-entity defer rationale: shipping a vocab name without Phase 3's actual semantic detail is a worse bet than waiting; PI-005 already tracks the work. Cost of defer: consultants who want to capture entity-touch data during Phase 1 fall back to freeform notes in process_notes, but Phase 1's official output does not require touches so the friction is small.
after_payload.title: process relationship architecture: direct FK for domain, references-entity directional edge for handoffs, defer process-to-entity entirely

after_payload.alternatives_considered: Bulk-create endpoint (rejected — not in scope for v0.4; case for it has not surfaced). Inline-handoff convenience endpoint (rejected — diverges from v2's decomposed-reference posture). Include Domain column in v0.4 master pane (rejected — would render DOM-NNN identifiers; same posture as entity.md). Collapse Hands-off-to and Receives-from into one references sub-section (rejected — loses directional information). Lock create-dialog handoff flow now (rejected — UI-layer decision better coordinated with parallel entity decision at v0.4-build planning).
after_payload.consequences: process API surface is implementable without surprises against the v0.3 governance-entity patterns. UI is implementable as a ListDetailPanel subclass with the standard CRUD-dialog suite plus a ReferencesSection rendering bidirectional process_hands_off_to_process edges in two named sub-sections. Acceptance criteria #10 through #15 give the v0.4 build concrete UI tests. The Domain-column deferral creates one v0.5+ follow-on PI (parallel to PI-009 but for the Processes panel) if CBM redo signal warrants — not authored now to avoid premature PI proliferation. Create-dialog handoff flow open question gets resolved at v0.4-build planning, coordinated with the parallel entity affiliations question.
after_payload.context: The process methodology entity needs a REST API surface, a desktop UI panel, and concrete acceptance criteria for v0.4 build. Defaults from domain.md and entity.md are honored unless deviation is methodology-driven; the spec guide section 3.5 (API), 3.6 (UI), and 3.7 (acceptance criteria) provide the template.
after_payload.decision: Standard endpoint set: GET /processes (with ?include_deleted=true), GET /processes/{identifier}, POST /processes, PUT /processes/{identifier}, PATCH /processes/{identifier}, DELETE /processes/{identifier} (soft-delete), POST /processes/{identifier}/restore, GET /processes/next-identifier. No deviations. Server-side classification-transition validation per section 3.4.2; server-side domain-FK validation rejecting references to non-existent or soft-deleted domains. Decomposed reference handling for handoffs: separate POST /references calls with source_type=process, target_type=process, relationship_kind=process_hands_off_to_process. No inline-handoff convenience endpoint. UI: Methodology sidebar group position #3 (Domains, Entities, Processes, CRM Candidates — all four ship together in v0.4). Master pane columns Identifier / Name / Classification / Updated, sorted by Identifier ascending. Domain column deferred to v0.5+ paired with PI-007 short codes, mirroring entity.md's deferral posture (PI-009 tracks the entity-side column; if the CBM redo reveals the Processes panel needs an analogous column, a follow-on PI is authored at that time). Detail pane vertical layout with fields in section 3.2 order; ReferencesSection renders bidirectional process_hands_off_to_process references in separate Hands-off-to and Receives-from sub-sections. Create-dialog handoff flow (create-then-attach vs create-with-attach) left as a v0.4-build decision matching the parallel open question on entity affiliations. 15 testable acceptance criteria cover migration, validation, access-layer methods, REST endpoints, identifier helper, soft-delete/restore, sidebar position, master pane, detail pane, CRUD dialogs, file-watch, vocab registration, references round-trip, sample CBM Phase 1 backbone authoring.
after_payload.rationale: No-deviation default endpoints minimize cross-spec complexity. Server-side validation (classification-transition, domain-FK) prevents UI-only enforcement bugs from corrupting engagement-scope state. Decomposed reference handling keeps the API consistent with v2's references-first discipline (DEC-006) and matches the entity.md / domain.md precedent. UI sidebar position #3 follows workstream order. Master pane Domain-column deferral mirrors entity.md (DEC-054) — without short codes the column renders DOM-NNN identifiers that don't tell a consultant anything at a glance, and the batched-join query is design work not yet validated against real-engagement signal. Detail pane separate Hands-off-to and Receives-from sub-sections honor the directional semantics of process_hands_off_to_process — collapsing them into a single 'Related processes' list would lose the direction information consultants need to read the backbone correctly. Create-dialog handoff flow open question matches entity.md's posture rather than locking the choice without v0.4-build coordination on parallel UX.
after_payload.title: process API surface, UI defaults, and acceptance criteria for v0.4

after_payload.description: DEC-057 adopted process_classification (mission_critical / supporting / deferred / unclassified) as the methodology priority classification per Principle 3. During the conversation the user observed that this is a classification, not a conventional scalar priority, and the methodology's three buckets don't answer the implementation-ranking question — 'of the mission-critical processes, which do we build first?'. A separate scalar implementation-priority field (working name process_priority, values Critical / High / Medium / Low or 1-5 or similar) is deferred to v0.5+. The deferral reasoning: Phase 1 does not formally produce per-process implementation priority — that information emerges from Phase 2 Slice Planning, which v0.4 schema does not yet host as content. When Phase 2 (Slice Planning) iteration content lands in v0.5+, the scalar priority field is part of that work, scoped per-iteration rather than global to the process record (the same process might have different scalar priorities in different iterations). Implementation when this PI lands: (a) add process_priority field with chosen value space; (b) decide whether the field is global on the process record or per-iteration via a join table to a future Iteration entity; (c) UI: master-pane column showing classification + priority paired (e.g., 'mission_critical / High'); (d) priority-transition validation if any (probably free movement per Principle 7). Prioritization signal: CBM redo Phase 1 will reveal whether consultants want implementation-priority ranking at Phase 1 cadence (probably no — Phase 1 outputs classification, not implementation order). Real signal comes from Phase 2 when slice planning content needs to host iteration-membership decisions.
after_payload.title: Future scalar implementation-priority field on process for ranking within classification buckets

after_payload.artifacts_produced:
PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/crm_candidate.md (the crm_candidate methodology entity schema specification, v1.0, 452 lines). Fourth and final spec in the methodology-schema-specs/ directory introduced by SES-012.

PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/ui-PRD-v0.4-build-planning-kickoff.md (the seed prompt for the fifth and final workstream conversation, v1.0, 210 lines). Authored alongside the schema spec per the schema-design kickoff's instructions for this fourth conversation.

Five decisions:
- DEC-060: crm_candidate identifier prefix and format (CRM, three letters)
- DEC-061: crm_candidate field inventory under minimum-viable v0.4 scope (Option A — thinnest)
- DEC-062: crm_candidate lifecycle, four-status enum with three terminal states, singleton-selected constraint, and removed-status deviation from rejection-via-soft-delete
- DEC-063: crm_candidate relationship posture and ENTITY_TYPES expansion (no new vocab kinds)
- DEC-064: crm_candidate API surface, UI defaults, singleton-selected enforcement, and acceptance criteria for v0.4 (12 testable statements)

One new planning item:
- PI-012: crm_candidate structured-metadata enums (vendor_url, hosting_type, license_type, price_tier) for v0.5+ pending CBM-redo signal

This session record (SES-015).

Five decided_in references (DEC-060..DEC-064 → SES-015).

Git commits on crmbuilder main: 4d5863a (crm_candidate.md v1.0 + ui-PRD-v0.4-build-planning-kickoff.md initial draft, both committed and ready for Doug to push). Close-out payload commit forthcoming with this JSON file and the companion apply prompt.
after_payload.conversation_reference: Claude.ai schema-design conversation on 05-12-26 that produced PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/crm_candidate.md v1.0 and PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/ui-PRD-v0.4-build-planning-kickoff.md, both committed to main at 4d5863a.
